# Set up Confluence Cloud scanning

There are two ways to set up scanning for Confluence Cloud -
- Import a single Confluence Cloud organization as a data source.
- Import many Confluence Cloud organizations together using a data source host.

To import a single Confluence Cloud organization as a data source, click **Settings**, then click **Data Sources**.
![Import single Confluence Cloud organization as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/confluence-saas/confluence_saas_data_source.png)

To import many Confluence Cloud organizations together as a data source host, click **Settings**, then click **Data Source Hosts**.
![Import many Confluence Cloud organizations as a data source host](/img/docs/vault-radar/confluence-saas/confluence_saas_data_source_host.png)

Additionally, for either option, you can choose to scan with **HCP Vault Radar Scan** or with the **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

# Connect to Confluence Cloud

For either the data source or data source host option, scanning can be set up using **HCP Vault Radar Scan**
or using **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

## Prerequisites

- Token creator email address
- [API token](https://support.atlassian.com/atlassian-account/docs/manage-api-tokens-for-your-atlassian-account/)

## Scan with HCP Vault Radar Scan

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**, then select **Confluence Cloud**.

  ![Add confluence SaaS data source with HCP](/img/docs/vault-radar/confluence-saas/confluence_saas_data_source_hcp.png)

1. If there is an **Enter your Confluence organization name** text field due to selecting the data source import workflow,
skip it for now to fill out in the [data source workflow](#import-a-data-source).

1. Enter the email address of the Confluence user that created the API token in
   the **Enter your Confluence token creator email** text field.

1. Enter the API token in the **Enter your Confluence API token** text field.

1. Then continue with the steps to [import a data source](#import-a-data-source) or []import a data source host](#import-a-data-source-host).

## Scan with Vault Radar Agent Scan

1. Make sure that the agent pool has been set up, refer to the [agent pool documentation](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) for more information.

@include 'vault-radar/add-group-to-resource.mdx'

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**, then select **Confluence Cloud**.

  ![Add confluence SaaS data source with agent](/img/docs/vault-radar/confluence-saas/confluence_saas_data_source_agent.png)

1. If there is an **Enter your Confluence organization name** text field due to selecting the data source import workflow,
skip it for now to fill out in the [data source workflow](#import-a-data-source)

1. Create an environment variable for the API token in the agent environment. This should be in the format `token_creator_email:api_token`.

   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```shell-session
   $ export CONFLUENCE_TOKEN=myemail@example.com:mytoken
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Enter the API token variable in the **Environment Path** text field with the `env://` prefix.

   **Example:**

   ```plaintext
   env://CONFLUENCE_TOKEN
   ```

# Import a data source

1. After having set up the connection details via **HCP Vault Radar Scan** or **Vault Radar Agent Scan**, enter the
Confluence Cloud organization name in the **Enter your Confluence organization name** text field.

1. Click **Next**

1. Select either the **All active spaces** or **Select spaces to monitor** radio
   button.

     Option 1 ![Add Confluence SaaS all](/img/docs/vault-radar/confluence-saas/confluence_saas_3.png)
     Option 2 ![Add Confluence SaaS selected](/img/docs/vault-radar/confluence-saas/confluence_saas_4.png)

1. Click **Finish**. Your Confluence Cloud spaces will now begin scanning.

# Import a data source host

1. Pass

# Related documentation

Refer to the [Confluence Cloud webhooks
documentation](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/get-started/add-data-sources/confluence/confluence-webhooks)
to enable webhook scanning.
